vitals.terrain: Road with mixed surfaces including pavé sections
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple ristori along course and at finish

course_description.overview: L'Étape Mondovì is a gran fondo held on September 20, 2026, featuring a 110.6 km route with 2271 m elevation gain through the hills between Mondovì and Alta Langa. The event is part of the prestigious L'Étape Italy circuit, affiliated with the Tour de France, and emphasizes closed roads, professional organization, and recreational cycling in a scenic Piedmont setting.

key_segments.name: Alta Langa Hills
key_segments.description: Multiple climbs through the Alta Langa region with pavé sections near Vicoforte Sanctuary

key_segments.name: Mondovì to Alta Langa
key_segments.description: Rolling terrain connecting Mondovì with Alta Langa, featuring 7 categorized climbs with maximum gradient of 17.2%

course_description.difficulty_narrative: The course presents moderate to challenging difficulty with seven distinct climbs, the steepest reaching 17.2% gradient. The 2271 m elevation gain over 110.6 km yields an average gradient of 2.05%, indicating sustained climbing rather than explosive pitches. Pavé sections add technical difficulty and require bike handling skills. The event is designed for experienced recreational cyclists with solid fitness.

logistics.getting_there: Mondovì is located in Cuneo province, Piedmont, Italy. Access via regional roads or rail connections to the Piedmont region. Specific transportation details not provided in official sources.
logistics.accommodation: Mondovì and surrounding Alta Langa region offer accommodation options. The event weekend (September 19-21, 2026) includes festival activities, suggesting local hospitality infrastructure. Specific recommendations not detailed in official materials.
logistics.registration_details: Registration open until September 17, 2026, with reopening on-site September 20-21. Pricing: €50 (until July 21), €60 (July 22-September 17), €70 (on-site), €75 (first grid, 200 spots). Maximum 2000 participants. Registration via ENDU platform requires valid cycling federation membership (FCI, ACSI, or recognized sports promotion body), medical fitness certificate valid through race date, and civil liability insurance. Italian residents without licenses can purchase day passes.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bikes (BDC) required. No specific bike transport or rental services mentioned in official sources. Mechanical support and recovery vehicles available for participants who abandon.
logistics.local_considerations: Event held September 20-21, 2026 (early autumn). Grandfondo start 9:30 AM, cicloturistica start 10:30 AM. Full road closures for safety. Medical assistance, showers, and commemorative medals provided. The event emphasizes family-friendly atmosphere with expo areas, street food, and entertainment beyond the race itself.

final_verdict.one_liner: A prestigious, well-organized gran fondo through stunning Piedmont hills with Tour de France-affiliated credibility and strong community atmosphere.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced recreational cyclists seeking a challenging but achievable gran fondo with professional organization, scenic beauty, and international prestige. Ideal for riders comfortable with 110+ km distances, sustained climbing, and technical pavé sections.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, particularly for cyclists valuing prestige and organization. The L'Étape Italy affiliation and Tour de France connection elevate the experience beyond typical regional gran fondos. Registration fees are reasonable (€50-70), though travel and accommodation costs should be factored in. The event's inaugural success (2000+ participants) and expanded 2026 format suggest strong quality and community engagement.
pro_tips: Register early (by July 21) to secure the €50 rate and guarantee entry before the 2000-person cap fills
Arrive September 19 to explore the festival atmosphere, expo, and local attractions in Alta Langa
Ensure medical fitness certificate is current and all federation documentation is uploaded to ENDU before race day
Practice pavé handling on rough surfaces before the event, as these sections require specific technique and can impact overall time
